LOCAL EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT SURVEY INDICATED A COUPLE HUNDRED TREES DOWN IN THE BLUE BEND AREA ALONG ANTHONY ROAD ABOUT TWO MILES WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF WV92 AND ANTHONY ROAD...OVER ABOUT A 1000 YARD PATH. MOST TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SOME SNAPPED AND ALMOST ALL LAYING IN DIRECTION OF STORM MOVEMENT OR SPREAD IN DIVERGENT PATTERN. MANY OF THESE TREES WERE IN A CAMPGROUND...BUT NO DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AND NO ONE WAS INJURED.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN IN THE BLUE BEND AREA ALONG ANTHONY ROAD ABOUT TWO MILES WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF WV92 AND ANTHONY ROAD.

A PORTION OF ANTHONY ROAD IN THE BLUE BEND AREA ABOUT TWO MILES WEST OF ITS INTERSECTION WITH WV92 WAS CLOSED BRIEFLY AS A RESULT OF HEAVY RAIN AND MUD THAT WASHED OUT ON THE ROAD. THE WATER RECEDED QUICKLY AND THERE WAS NO DAMAGE REPORTED TO THE ROAD.
